acceptance.reason: xlog_total_train_sec is 43.9x scallop_total_train_sec on the same run matrix

task: MNIST addition (5 epochs, 512 train images, batch_size=64)

notes: Result is not acceptable for Track B closure: XLOG is ~44x slower on total train time.
Per-query device synchronize() is no longer the cause; that barrier was removed in prior perf fixes.
Current bottleneck is dominated by sequential per-query circuit evaluation overhead inside each training batch.
Both frameworks are at early training (5 epochs, 512 images) — neither has converged.
Accuracy difference is within noise at this training budget.

hardware: NVIDIA RTX PRO 3000 Blackwell Generation Laptop GPU (12 GB, SM120)
